quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 9 Quiz 3
quiz.subtitle: Electric Current — Charge, Time, and Conductors
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A device used to detect the presence and magnitude of electric charge is called a(n):
questions.explanation: An electroscope detects and indicates the presence and magnitude of static electric charge. An ammeter measures electric current, a voltmeter measures electrical potential difference, and an electrode is a conductor used to establish electrical contact with a nonmetallic part of a circuit.

questions.question: A current of $3.0\text{ A}$ flows for $5.0\text{ s}$. How much charge passes through the circuit?
A charge of $48\text{ C}$ passes through a wire in $8.0\text{ s}$. What is the current?
How long must a current of $2.5\text{ A}$ flow to deliver $75\text{ C}$ of charge?
A current of $4.0\text{ A}$ flows for $2.0$ minutes. How much charge passes?
A current of $250\text{ mA}$ flows for $10\text{ s}$. How much charge passes?
Which of the following is the best electrical conductor?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal with many free electrons, making it an excellent conductor. Glass, plastic, and wood are insulators.

questions.question: Which of the following is the best electrical insulator?
questions.explanation: Glass has tightly bound electrons that cannot move freely. Silver and aluminum are conductors; graphite is an unusual non-metal conductor.

questions.question: Metals are good conductors of electricity because they contain:
questions.explanation: Metals have a 'sea' of delocalized electrons that are free to move throughout the structure, carrying charge.

questions.question: Insulators do not conduct electricity well because:
options: They contain more protons than electrons
They have larger gaps between their atoms
questions.explanation: In insulators, electrons are held tightly to their atoms and cannot flow. All matter contains electrons.

questions.question: Which of these would conduct electricity?
questions.explanation: Graphite conducts electricity due to delocalized electrons between its carbon layers. Distilled water lacks ions, timber is an insulator, and diamond has all electrons tightly bound in covalent bonds.

questions.question: The electric force between two charges is strongest when the charges are:
questions.explanation: According to Coulomb's Law, $F \propto 1/r^2$. The smaller the distance between charges, the larger the force. Force is maximum when charges are closest together.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered charge, current, time calculations and conductor identification!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review unit conversions (mA to A, minutes to seconds) and why graphite conducts.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on $Q = It$ and identifying conductors vs. insulators.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: $Q = It$, the definition of current, and what makes materials conduct or insulate.
